title: Share of artificial intelligence jobs among all job postings
subtitle: A job posting is considered an AI job if it requests one or more AI skills, e.g., "natural language processing", "neural networks", "machine learning", or "robotics".
caption:
Share of artificial intelligence jobs among all job postings

A job posting is considered an AI job if it requests one or more AI skills, e.g., "natural language processing", "neural networks", "machine learning", or "robotics".

The line chart tracks the percentage of job postings requiring artificial intelligence skills across eight countries from 2014 to 2024. The United States consistently maintains the highest share, starting near 0.6% in 2014 and rising to a peak of 1.8% in 2024, with a notable dip to 1.4% in 2023. The United Kingdom and Sweden follow similar upward trajectories, both reaching approximately 1.3% by 2024 after starting around 0.4% and 0.9% respectively in their earlier recorded years. The Netherlands, Spain, and Austria cluster in the middle range, generally fluctuating between 1.0% and 1.3% from 2019 onward, with Spain showing a sharp drop to roughly 0.95% in 2023 before recovering. Italy’s share remains lower, hovering between 0.5% and 0.9% over the period shown. New Zealand records the lowest values throughout, beginning below 0.2% in 2014 and ending near 0.55% in 2024, with minimal variation year to year. All countries exhibit an overall increasing trend in AI job postings as a share of total postings, though the magnitude and timing of growth vary. The data spans from 2014 for the United States and New Zealand, while other countries begin reporting between 2019 and 2020. The vertical axis ranges from 0% to 1.8%, marked at 0.2% intervals, and the horizontal axis covers annual increments from 2014 through 2024.
